name: Faith Community United
description_short: Faith Community United is a state-chartered credit union in Cleveland, Ohio, serving over 4,500 members with $15.4M in assets and offering standard banking products.
description_long:
Faith Community United Credit Union was established in 1983 and is headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. The institution operates as a state-chartered credit union with a single branch location at 3550 East 93rd Street. With over 4,500 members and approximately 10 dedicated employees, the credit union manages $15.4 million in assets, positioning it as a small community-focused financial institution.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial services. Members can access checking accounts (Share Draft products with 1,500+ accounts), savings accounts (4,000+ regular savings), money market accounts, certificates of deposit (52+ active CDs), personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and credit cards. Digital services include online banking, mobile app access, bill pay, mobile payments, e-statements, wire transfers, and financial calculators. The institution also provides IRA/KEOGH accounts and financial counseling services.

Faith Community United distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and accessibility to Ohio residents. The credit union operates with a membership eligibility structure tied to Ohio residency and offers personalized financial counseling and educational workshops. The organization maintains a physical presence with lobby hours (currently open until 2:30 pm) and provides traditional in-person banking services alongside digital options.

However, the institution faces significant operational and customer service challenges. Member reviews reveal consistent problems with online banking login failures, poor customer service responsiveness (members report difficulty reaching staff by phone), and service interruptions. The aggregated member rating stands at 1.6 out of 5 stars based on five reviews, with complaints about avoidance, miscommunication, and failure to follow through on commitments. While one positive review mentions good community roots, the overwhelming negative feedback regarding outdated systems and unreliable digital banking suggests substantial infrastructure limitations for a modern financial institution.
pros: Established community credit union since 1983 with deep local roots in Cleveland
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, CDs, and IRAs
Offers business loans in addition to personal banking products
Provides financial counseling and financial education workshops
Mobile app available for both iOS and Android
Electronic bill payment, wire transfers, and e-statements
Membership eligibility tailored to Ohio residents with community focus
cons: Severe online banking login issues and continuous password reset failures reported by multiple members
Poor phone customer service with members unable to reach staff
Very low member satisfaction rating of 1.6/5 stars with complaints about avoidance and dishonesty
Single branch location with no ATM infrastructure
Outdated systems acknowledged by members as limiting functionality
best_for: Ohio residents seeking a community-based credit union with personal relationships
Members who primarily use in-branch banking and avoid digital services
Businesses and individuals seeking local loan origination with face-to-face interaction
diagnosis: Faith Community United is best suited for Ohio residents who value community banking relationships and can conduct most business in-person, as the credit union's digital infrastructure suffers from significant reliability issues. Prospective members should be aware that online banking functionality is unreliable with recurring login and password reset failures, and phone customer service responsiveness is poor—making this institution problematic for those dependent on remote banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
